summary: Add the strict Release Manifest v1 data boundary and canonical identity core.
rationale: The first External Execution Plane implementation slice needs one typed, fail-closed manifest contract before signature, build closure, or installer work can consume release metadata.
verification: uv run ruff check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
deletion_targets: Hand-written release canonicalization and untyped manifest parsing paths
risks: Later R1b signature verification must consume these exact canonical bytes without adding a second parser or canonicalizer.
Filesystem closure and installer behavior remain intentionally outside this pure-data R1a slice.
dependency_rationale: RFC 8785 number and string canonicalization is delegated to the pinned pure-Python rfc8785 0.1.4 implementation instead of being reimplemented locally.
